title.en: Simulate a Capital One ATM session state machine
title.es: Simular una máquina de estados de sesión ATM de Capital One

statement.en:
A Capital One ATM session passes through four states: `"IDLE"`, `"AWAITING_PIN"`, `"AUTHENTICATED"`, and `"LOCKED"`. The machine also maintains a private `pinFails` counter that tracks failed PIN attempts since the last time the counter was reset.

Given a `String[] events` where each element is one of `"INSERT_CARD"`, `"PIN_OK"`, `"PIN_FAIL"`, `"WITHDRAW"`, `"DEPOSIT"`, or `"EJECT"`, simulate the state machine starting from `"IDLE"` and return the **final state** as a `String`.

Signature: `public String finalState(String[] events)`

Unknown event strings are no-ops. No exceptions are thrown.

**Transition table:**

| Current state | Event | Next state | Side effect |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IDLE` | `INSERT_CARD` | `AWAITING_PIN` | — |
| `IDLE` | any other | `IDLE` | — |
| `AWAITING_PIN` | `PIN_OK` | `AUTHENTICATED` | reset `pinFails` to 0 |
| `AWAITING_PIN` | `PIN_FAIL` (3rd total fail) | `LOCKED` | — |
| `AWAITING_PIN` | `PIN_FAIL` (fewer than 3 fails) | `AWAITING_PIN` | increment `pinFails` |
| `AWAITING_PIN` | `EJECT` | `IDLE` | reset `pinFails` to 0 |
| `AWAITING_PIN` | any other | `AWAITING_PIN` | — |
| `AUTHENTICATED` | `WITHDRAW` or `DEPOSIT` | `AUTHENTICATED` | — |
| `AUTHENTICATED` | `EJECT` | `IDLE` | reset `pinFails` to 0 |
| `AUTHENTICATED` | any other | `AUTHENTICATED` | — |
| `LOCKED` | any event | `LOCKED` | terminal state |

The `pinFails` counter resets to `0` on `EJECT` from either `AWAITING_PIN` or `AUTHENTICATED`, and on a successful `PIN_OK`.

Examples:
- `["INSERT_CARD", "PIN_FAIL", "PIN_FAIL", "PIN_FAIL"]` → `"LOCKED"` (3 consecutive fails)
- `["INSERT_CARD", "PIN_FAIL", "EJECT", "INSERT_CARD", "PIN_FAIL", "PIN_FAIL"]` → `"AWAITING_PIN"` (EJECT resets counter; only 2 fails after re-insert)
- `["INSERT_CARD", "PIN_OK", "WITHDRAW", "DEPOSIT", "EJECT"]` → `"IDLE"`
- `[]` → `"IDLE"`
statement.es:
Una sesión ATM de Capital One pasa por cuatro estados: `"IDLE"`, `"AWAITING_PIN"`, `"AUTHENTICATED"` y `"LOCKED"`. La máquina también mantiene un contador privado `pinFails` que registra los intentos fallidos de PIN desde la última vez que se reinició el contador.

Dado un arreglo `String[] events` donde cada elemento es uno de `"INSERT_CARD"`, `"PIN_OK"`, `"PIN_FAIL"`, `"WITHDRAW"`, `"DEPOSIT"` o `"EJECT"`, simula la máquina de estados comenzando desde `"IDLE"` y devuelve el **estado final** como `String`.

Firma: `public String finalState(String[] events)`

Las cadenas de eventos desconocidas son no-op. No se lanzan excepciones.

**Tabla de transiciones:**

| Estado actual | Evento | Estado siguiente | Efecto secundario |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IDLE` | `INSERT_CARD` | `AWAITING_PIN` | — |
| `IDLE` | cualquier otro | `IDLE` | — |
| `AWAITING_PIN` | `PIN_OK` | `AUTHENTICATED` | reiniciar `pinFails` a 0 |
| `AWAITING_PIN` | `PIN_FAIL` (3.er fallo total) | `LOCKED` | — |
| `AWAITING_PIN` | `PIN_FAIL` (menos de 3 fallos) | `AWAITING_PIN` | incrementar `pinFails` |
| `AWAITING_PIN` | `EJECT` | `IDLE` | reiniciar `pinFails` a 0 |
| `AWAITING_PIN` | cualquier otro | `AWAITING_PIN` | — |
| `AUTHENTICATED` | `WITHDRAW` o `DEPOSIT` | `AUTHENTICATED` | — |
| `AUTHENTICATED` | `EJECT` | `IDLE` | reiniciar `pinFails` a 0 |
| `AUTHENTICATED` | cualquier otro | `AUTHENTICATED` | — |
| `LOCKED` | cualquier evento | `LOCKED` | estado terminal |

El contador `pinFails` se reinicia a `0` al hacer `EJECT` desde `AWAITING_PIN` o `AUTHENTICATED`, y al recibir un `PIN_OK` exitoso.

Ejemplos:
- `["INSERT_CARD", "PIN_FAIL", "PIN_FAIL", "PIN_FAIL"]` → `"LOCKED"` (3 fallos consecutivos)
- `["INSERT_CARD", "PIN_FAIL", "EJECT", "INSERT_CARD", "PIN_FAIL", "PIN_FAIL"]` → `"AWAITING_PIN"` (EJECT reinicia el contador; solo 2 fallos tras re-inserción)
- `["INSERT_CARD", "PIN_OK", "WITHDRAW", "DEPOSIT", "EJECT"]` → `"IDLE"`
- `[]` → `"IDLE"`
